title: RAG and Retrieval
description: Test your knowledge of retrieval-augmented generation and vector search

questions.question: What problem does Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) primarily address?
options: Grounding responses in external, up-to-date or private knowledge the model wasn't trained on
Making the model generate images
Reducing the number of GPUs needed to train a model
Translating between programming languages
questions.explanation: RAG retrieves relevant documents at query time and adds them to the prompt, letting the model answer from sources beyond its training data.

questions.question: What is an 'embedding' in the context of retrieval?
options: A numeric vector that represents the meaning of a piece of text
A compressed image file
A cached copy of the model weights
A type of API authentication token
questions.explanation: An embedding maps text to a vector so that semantically similar texts land close together, enabling similarity search.

questions.question: What does a vector database do in a RAG pipeline?
options: Stores embeddings and finds the most similar ones to a query vector
Renders the final HTML page
Fine-tunes the language model on a schedule
Manages user passwords
questions.explanation: A vector database indexes embeddings and performs nearest-neighbour search to return the passages most semantically relevant to the query.

questions.question: Why is document 'chunking' important before indexing for retrieval?
options: Splitting long documents into passages improves retrieval precision and fits context limits
It permanently deletes duplicate documents
It converts text to audio
It encrypts the documents
questions.explanation: Breaking documents into focused chunks means retrieval returns the specific relevant passage rather than a whole document, and each chunk fits the context window.

questions.question: How does RAG help reduce hallucinations?
options: By grounding the answer in retrieved source text the model can cite
By raising the temperature to maximum
By removing the system prompt
By shortening the user's question
questions.explanation: Providing authoritative retrieved context gives the model facts to rely on and cite, reducing the chance it fabricates an answer.
